title: Run Gemma on the edge with the Coral Board
summary: Google's Coral Board is a low-power development board with an integrated Coral NPU accelerator designed for on-device AI inference. It ships with a screen, camera, microphones, and LEDs, and supports running Gemma models for applications like live translation, natural language control, and multimodal AI workloads entirely on the board.
writeup: The video demonstrates running Gemma models on Google's Coral Board, a low-power development board with an integrated Coral NPU accelerator built for on-device AI inference. The board ships with a screen, camera, microphones, and LEDs, and it supports multimodal workloads such as live translation and natural language control executed entirely on the hardware. The main point: Gemma inference can run locally on the NPU without offloading to the cloud.
